content: SYSTEM UNDERSTANDING — TOOL 1 WIRING (UI + EXECUTION PATH)
What is Tool 1 Run in this system?
Tool 1 Run is the API testing execution path used to run HTTP system-eval tests. It is the mechanism that executes API calls, validates responses, and produces results.
Is Tool 1 connected to the UI?
Yes. Tool 1 is directly integrated into the UI. The UI provides controls for running tests and displays the results generated by Tool 1.
How does the UI connect to Tool 1?
The UI imports the operator and log modules and calls the execution function when a test is triggered. The main call is tool1_operator.run_tool1_system_eval_http(...).
What is system_eval_operator.py responsible for?
system_eval_operator.py acts as the execution wrapper. It receives input from the UI, prepares the test suite, and triggers the core evaluation engine.
What happens inside system_eval_operator when a test runs?
It loads the test suite, executes it through the system_eval engine, generates results, writes artifacts, and sends the results back to the UI.
What is core/system_eval.py responsible for?
core/system_eval.py is the core testing engine. It executes test cases, validates responses, measures results, and produces structured outputs.
What artifacts are generated during execution?
The system generates JSON and Markdown artifacts that contain detailed results of the test execution.
Where are test runs recorded?
Each run is recorded in an append-only log file located at logs/tool1_runs.jsonl.
What does tool1_run_log.py do?
tool1_run_log.py is responsible for writing structured records of each test run into the log file, including request details, results, and summaries.
What is the full execution flow of Tool 1?
The execution flow is:
UI → system_eval_operator.py → core/system_eval.py → artifacts → tool1_run_log.py → UI display.
Is Tool 1 part of the reasoning system?
No. Tool 1 is part of the execution layer. It performs actions, runs tests, and generates evidence. It does not perform reasoning.
What is the purpose of Tool 1 in Joshua’s system?
Tool 1 provides real-world execution, validation, and evidence. It allows Joshua to move from analysis to action and verify results through actual API testing.
SUMMARY: Tool 1 is the execution pipeline that connects the UI to the core testing engine and logging system, enabling API testing, result generation, and persistent run tracking.
